comment: Default-FAIL verification contract for domain-review-loop and domain-reviewer-a/b (T-005). Non-code stack (Markdown agent/skill/reference definitions plus one shell script) per specs/sdd-domain/tasks.md Global Constraints: lint/typecheck/build are waivable with waiver_reason instead of fabricated. placeholder-scan genuinely FAILS (passes:false) with 2 findings in plugins/sdd-domain/skills/domain-review-loop/SKILL.md, lines 69 and 106 -- both confirmed, on inspection, to be legitimate prose using the word 'placeholder' to describe real, fully-implemented logic (a normalized-hash substitution and a stable pseudo-feature-slug), not stub/TODO/NotImplementedError code, matching the false-positive scenario documented in deterministic-check-policy.md's 'check-placeholders Scope and Waivers' section. However, that policy's only remedy (required:false + waiver_reason + human acceptance) is blocked at the high-risk tier: check-contract.py's RISK_TIERS/COMPILE_CHECKS logic only allows {lint, typecheck, build} to be waived on a non-code stack; placeholder-scan is not in that set and check-contract rejects required:false for it at risk=high regardless of stack ('risk high requires check present and required:true'). This is left required:true/passes:false as the honest, real state; check-contract.sh therefore fails closed on this task as run. No plugins/ file was edited to resolve this (out of scope for this prep pass) and no verdict is asserted here.

checks.waiver_reason: Non-code stack (docs/shell): no lint command configured for Markdown, JSON Schema, or shell/PowerShell artifacts in this repository, per tasks.md Global Constraints.
Non-code stack (docs/shell): no statically typed build target exists for Markdown/JSON Schema/shell/PowerShell artifacts.
Non-code stack (docs/shell): no compiled-language build step applies to this task's files.

No cross-service/cross-process integration surface exists for this task; the precheck script's own process-boundary behavior (invoking bash.exe as a real subprocess) is exercised inside unit-tests, not as a separate integration suite.

No dev server, Docker container, or CLI binary entry point exists for this task's artifacts (agent/skill Markdown definitions and a precondition shell script invoked only by the review-loop skill, not a standalone runnable service).
No prior version of this review gate exists to diff against; this is new infrastructure, not a modification of existing runtime behavior.
No user-facing UI is in scope; this task is agent/skill/script infrastructure only.
No design-system/ directory applies to this task; no UI-layer files are touched.
